description: The adjective 'theru' applies when the distance between opposing surfaces or the breadth of a cross-section is substantial. It also covers material with dense or viscous body, as in 'theru loesi' (thick oil), and a form well filled out. Width is 'losha' (wide), a different measurement from thickness.
articulatory_notes: The tongue approaches the upper teeth for voiceless 'th', with air continuing into true-mid front 'e'. A tap or short trill begins the second syllable and releases into rounded high-back 'u'. Stress rests on 'the'.
usage_notes: The phrase 'theru moru' describes a thick wall, and 'theru nuko loesi' is thick black oil. For a wall, thickness runs between its faces; 'nulo' (deep) instead follows distance inward or downward.

semantic_domains.physical: substantial material thickness, density, or viscosity
semantic_domains.spatial: a relatively large distance between opposing surfaces or across a cross-section
